title: acacia excelsa subsp. angusta
intro: Acacia excelsa subsp. angusta, also known as the Philippine ironwood, is a subspecies of tree native to the Philippines and other parts of Southeast Asia, characterized by its hard, durable wood and small, fragrant flowers. The plant has been found to have various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other compounds. The health benefits of Acacia excelsa subsp. angusta include its potential in treating various ailments, such as wounds, fever, and digestive issues, as well as its use as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infusion, or used in herbal preparations such as tinctures and salves, to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
